title: Splunk SOAR + ISE Closed-Loop Playbook Audit and Mean-Time-to-Containment KPI

compliance.controlObjective: Mean-time-to-contain KPI for ANC-driven SOAR playbook executions.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.17 ICT-related incident management — automated containment efficacy.
SOC-2 CC7.4 incident response — automated containment.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Run a SOAR `block_endpoint` playbook. Confirm ISE 86001 ANC apply within 30s.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All playbooks succeed within target MTTC. No fire.

dataSources: Splunk SOAR audit logs `index=phantom` showing playbook runs, action results from the *Cisco ISE* SOAR app (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4250) — `block_endpoint`, `unblock_endpoint`, `endpoint_session_info` actions. Combine with ISE syslog 86001 (ANC-applied) to audit reach.
app: `Splunk SOAR Cisco ISE App` (Splunkbase 4250) + Splunk SOAR.
spl:
index=phantom action_name=block_endpoint
| eval soar_apply_time=_time
| stats earliest(soar_apply_time) as soar_t, latest(_time) as last_action by container_id, mac_address
| join mac_address [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=86001 anc_action=quarantine | rename _time as ise_apply_time]
| eval soar_to_ise_lag_s=ise_apply_time - soar_t
| stats avg(soar_to_ise_lag_s) as avg_lag, max(soar_to_ise_lag_s) as max_lag
description: Audits Splunk SOAR playbook → ISE ANC closed-loop performance. Measures (a) SOAR `block_endpoint` action success rate, (b) average lag from SOAR action to ISE 86001 confirmation, and (c) max lag (worst case).
value: Automated containment is only as fast as its slowest link. SOAR playbook audit gives the SOC measurable evidence that playbooks executed end-to-end, satisfying NIS2/DORA incident-response requirements with quantitative MTTC.
implementation: Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 24 hours; emit MTTC KPI to a summary index for the SOAR-effectiveness dashboard.
visualization: MTTC trend; per-playbook success rate; failed-action log.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) SOAR action retries cause apparent duplicate apply.
(b) Endpoints already quarantined return success without a new 86001.
mitreAttack: N/A (meta-detection — measures playbook execution KPIs, not adversary behaviour)
grandmaExplanation: Splunk SOAR is the automation that runs playbooks. When SOAR tells ISE to quarantine a device, we measure how long it takes — and prove the response actually happened.
